title: stizolobium macrophyllum
intro: Stizolobium macrophyllum, also known as the 'Brazilian Pea Tree' or 'Sensitive Plant', is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The leaves and stems of the plant have been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting immune function. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural antiseptic to clean wounds and prevent infection.
